summary: The latest visible arXiv batch on July 7 emphasized experiment-facing heavy-ion baselines, fluctuation-cumulant methodology, baryon-transport diagnostics, and detector-analysis AI/ML. The strongest heavy-ion item is the sPHENIX isolated prompt photon cross-section measurement, because it establishes a 200 GeV p+p reference for future heavy-ion photon measurements. SAM-3.0 remains the strongest phenomenology/statistical-methods contribution because it gives validated exact-conservation corrections for cumulants relevant to RHIC-BES fluctuation analyses. The strongest AI/ML-transfer papers are flow-based likelihood surrogates for neutrino-systematics inference and RTL fault injection for a deployed graph-neural-network trigger; both address production analysis reliability rather than generic benchmark performance. Two vorticity/spin-signature papers are flagged for caution because their abstracts make broad claims with limited visible validation and overlap strongly with each other.
strongest_paper_summary: The strongest experiment-facing heavy-ion paper is the sPHENIX isolated prompt photon measurement because it provides the p+p reference needed for future heavy-ion photon analyses; the strongest methods paper is SAM-3.0 because it supplies validated exact-conservation corrections for fluctuation cumulants.

papers.title: Measurement of isolated prompt photon production in p+p collisions at sqrt(s) = 200 GeV with the sPHENIX detector
papers.authors: sPHENIX Collaboration: M. I. Abdulhamid, U. Acharya, G. Adawi, I. Ahmed, C. A. Aidala et al.
papers.abstract: sPHENIX measures the isolated prompt photon differential cross section versus photon transverse energy in 200 GeV p+p collisions, compares the result with PYTHIA and NLO/NNLO pQCD, and finds consistency within quoted uncertainties.

papers.title: Subensemble Acceptance Method 3.0: General Corrections to Cumulants from Exact Conservation Constraints
papers.authors: Roman Poberezhniuk, Volodymyr A. Kuznietsov, Gregoire Pihan, Volodymyr Vovchenko
papers.abstract: SAM-3.0 corrects subsystem cumulants for exact global conservation of multiple charges, including energy; it gives a closed recursion, recovers earlier SAM limits, fixes binomial-acceptance behavior, updates Hydro-EV net-proton baselines, and validates against direct canonical Monte Carlo sampling.

papers.title: Studying baryon number transport dynamics via hyperon-kaon correlations in p+Au collisions at sqrt(s_NN)=20, 39 and 62 GeV
papers.authors: Siyuan Ping, Xiaozhou Yu, Long Ma
papers.abstract: The paper proposes hyperon-kaon rapidity correlations as a probe of baryon-number transport and strange-quark pair correlations, using AMPT and UrQMD as baselines without a baryon-junction mechanism.

papers.title: Evaluation of U-235 and U-238 Fission Product Yields Using Bayesian Neural Networks: Comparison of Baseline and Physics-Informed Models
papers.authors: Chun-Yuan Qiao, Ya-Xuan Wang, Jun-Chen Pei, Chun-Wang Ma, Yong-Jing Chen et al.
papers.abstract: A Bayesian neural-network model for cumulative fission yields is improved with physics-informed features including odd-even effects, beta-decay energy, and isospin, narrowing confidence intervals and improving agreement with data and evaluated libraries for representative products.

papers.title: Flow-Based Surrogates for High-Dimensional Likelihoods in Experimental Neutrino Physics
papers.authors: Mathias El Baz, Lorenzo Giannessi, Adrien Blanchet, Federico Sanchez
papers.abstract: The authors train a hybrid normalizing-flow architecture on a 110-parameter near-detector likelihood with explicit non-Gaussian parameters and report 98% relative effective sample size versus about 5% for a Gaussian approximation while reproducing an MCMC reference.

papers.title: RTL Fault Injection of a Deployed Graph Neural Network Trigger for Belle II
papers.authors: Georgios Sotiropoulos, Marc Neu, Tanja Harbaum, Torben Ferber, Jurgen Becker
papers.abstract: The paper performs RTL-level fault injection on Belle II's deployed GNN-ETM trigger, injecting 1,442,840 single-event upsets across 211,245 signals and showing that output-only monitoring can misestimate mean time to failure by up to 78.7%.

papers.title: Neural-Network-Assisted Binary Template Construction for Matrix-Based Pattern Matching in the STCF MDC
papers.authors: Zhaoli Guo, Qingyu Li, Baolin Zhang, Jiarui Zhao, Aonan Zhu, Liang Peng, Huilin Li
papers.abstract: A neural network is used offline to optimize binary template libraries for fast bitwise pattern matching in a drift chamber; no neural inference is required online, preserving deterministic trigger speed while improving template coverage in simulation.

papers.title: ML and AI for density functional theory: different priorities for Kohn-Sham and orbital-free DFT, for electronic and nuclear DFT
papers.abstract: This review contrasts ML priorities for Kohn-Sham and orbital-free DFT across electronic and nuclear contexts, arguing that symbolic regression may be especially attractive for kinetic-energy functionals and nuclear DFT transfer.

papers.title: Vorticity in the Quark-Gluon Plasma: Volumetric Expansion, Regularization, and Spin Signatures
papers.authors: Malak Ait Tamlihat, Ghizlane Ez-Zobayr, Laurent Schoeffel, Yahya Tayalati
papers.abstract: The paper presents a self-contained treatment of QGP vorticity, volumetric expansion, and spin signatures, including a claim that expansion regularizes vortex self-amplification and that global mid-rapidity Lambda polarization can vanish under a peripheral dipole topology.
